project.title: Quantitative proteomics of epithelial mesenchymal transition of human primary small airway epithelial cells: part 1
project.projectDescription: A study employs quantitative proteomics approach fro systems biology understanding of the epithelial mesenchymal transition of human primary small airway epithelial cells.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The sample processing included protein extraction, protein digestion, iTRAQ labeling, strong cation exchange chromatography, and LC-MS/MS
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Thermo Scientific Proteome Discoverer 1.4 for protein identification and quantification
keywords: Human primary small airway epithelial cells

references.referenceLine: Zhao Y, Tian B, Sadygov RG, Zhang Y, Brasier AR. Integrative proteomic analysis reveals reprograming tumor necrosis factor signaling in epithelial mesenchymal transition. J Proteomics. 2016 148:126-138

identifiedPTMStrings.name: No PTMs are included in the dataset

Characteristics[Organism]: primary human small airway epithelial cells (hSAECs)
Characteristics[MaterialType]: An immortalized primary human small airway epithelial cell line
Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: Each experimental group has two biological replicates.
Characteristics[NumberOfSamples]: quantified across all the eight samples
Comment[Instrument]: Q Exactive Orbitrap mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific, San Jose, CA)
Comment[FractionationMethod]: strong cation exchange chromatography of iTRAQ labeled peptides
Comment[NumberOfFractions]: 60 fractions were collected throughout the run
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: A Fe-NTA phosphopeptide enrichment kit (Thermo Scientific-Pierce) was used for enrichment of phosphopeptides.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: maximum of two missed cleavage sites permitted
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: a mass tolerance of ± 20 ppm for precursor and product ions
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: a mass tolerance of ± 20 ppm for precursor and product ions
